Research in the Journal of Positive Psychology shows that people who practice disengagement coping, letting go of unchangeable people or situations, report higher well-being and less depression than those who continue trying to fix. People are not yours to fix. People are not yours to solve. People are not yours to change. Instead of disrespecting them, first start with distance. And if distance doesn't work, then disconnect.
